course_title: Foundations for Social Work Practice in Disability

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the lived experience of disability as it relates to individuals, families, and communities.
Critically analyse theoretical frameworks for understanding disability.
Describe and analyse the social, political, legal and organisational contexts of social work practice in the disability field both locally and internationally.
Identify and critically apply social work interventions that are flexible and responsive to people with disabilities, families and communities.

assessments.weighting: <dd>30% each quiz worth 5%; best 6 out of 7 quizzes included in count </dd>

assessments.task_description:
In weeks 2-5 and 9, 11, and 12, you will undertake a brief (15 minute; 4-5 questions) in person quiz during the workshop. Each quiz is worth 5% of your final grade. The best 6 out of 7 quizzes will count towards your mark out of 30.

We will do a practice quiz in Week 1 to familiarise yourself with assessment requirements.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This description is only a summary of the task. For full details, including the marking rubric, please refer to the course Learn UQ (Blackboard) site.

This task requires you to develop a practice response to assist a person with a disability to access supports under the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS).  A fictitious case scenario will be provided to you at the beginning of semester via Blackboard. You will be required to answer a series of questions. Using a critical disability lens, you will provide an analysis of the situation for the person and their family in this case scenario. You will also write an account of your practice approach. 

This task also has an interprofessional component; on Thursday 17th September, 2-4pm students will participate in a workshop with speech pathology students to discuss the case and practice approaches. Your summary of your approach as a social worker will include some discussion about how you would work with a speech pathologist to assist the person and family. Your assessment should also make reference to relevant sources (including quality academic and grey literature, relevant websites).

Details of the case study will be provided on Blackboard early in the semester.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment requires students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard. The electronic version of your assignment will be submitted via Blackboard, using the Turnitin software.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This description is only a summary of the task. For full details, including the marking rubric, please refer to the course Learn UQ (Blackboard) site.

Overview: Your task for this assessment is to design an Easy Information Sheet about a community service and reflect on your process of developing the information and how the service could be more accessible to people with intellectual disabilities.

This assessment supports the development of inclusive communication practices and aligns with Article 21 of the United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities, which recognises the right of people with disabilities to accessible information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment requires students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard. The electronic version of your assignment will be submitted via Blackboard, using the Turnitin software.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1: Introduction to the field of disability

Thursday 30th July: This workshop is a beginning exploration of the impacts of disability and disablement on individuals, families and communities. Definitions and theories around disability are introduced and students will be encouraged to reflect upon their own personal values, understandings and experiences of difference, and their use of language and discourse. We will also have a practice run of doing the in-class quiz.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 1: Introduction to the field of disability

Definitions and theories around disability are introduced and students will be encouraged to reflect upon their own personal values, understandings and experiences of difference, and their use of language and discourse.  We will also have a practice run of doing the in-class quiz.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2: Further exploration of disability theory

Thursday, 6th August: This week we delve further into disability theory, reconciling the tensions between individual and social analyses of the disability experience. We will conclude the session with your first of seven (1/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade). It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content. Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02

We will conclude the session with your first of seven (1/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade).  It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content.  Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 3: Disability History in the Global North

Thursday, 13th August: This week we will explore a recent history of social responses to disability in Australia and other "developed" countries. In particular, we will look at the positioning of people with a disability from European colonisation until the present day in Australia.

We will conclude the session with your second of seven (2/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade). It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content. Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4: The National Disability Insurance Scheme

Thursday, 20th August: The National Disability Insurance Scheme is the major program which provides supports to people with a disability and their families in Australia. This week we will cover the major principles and processes behind the scheme, the latest policy developments and discuss implications for practice.

We will conclude the session with your third of seven (3/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade). It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content. Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04

Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5: Communicating with people with disabilities

Thursday, 27th August: This workshop introduces core knowledge and skills for communicating with people with disabilities. It emphasises respectful language and explores a range of disability conditions and impairments. While basic clinical information is covered, it is approached cautiously, recognising that each individual experiences their disability in unique ways.

We will conclude the session with your fourth of seven (4/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade). It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content. Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Week 6: Person-centred practice

Thursday, 3rd September: This workshop covers a number of community-minded practices which focus on the strengths and aspirations of people with disabilities. These approaches encourage us to move away from service-minded thinking to consider natural supports and freely-given relationships for people with disabilities.

There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the practice response assessment.

Learning outcomes: L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6: Person-centred practice Thursday, 3rd September: This workshop covers a number of community-minded practices which focus on the strengths and aspirations of people with disabilities. These approaches encourage us to move away from service-minded thinking to consider natural supports and freely-given relationships for people with disabilities. There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the practice response assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7: Family-centred practice

Thursday, 10th September: This workshop builds on the learning from the previous week, considering how to work effectively with families who have a person with a disability. We will consider a life course approach to working with families, explore the natural authority of family members and discuss the complexities of being family-centred practitioners.

There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the practice response assessment.

Learning outcomes: L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7: Family-centred practice Thursday, 10th September: This workshop builds on the learning from the previous week, considering how to work effectively with families who have a person with a disability. We will consider a life course approach to working with families, explore the natural authority of family members and discuss the complexities of being family-centred practitioners. There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the practice response assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8: Interprofessional practice (Workshop in the afternoon)

Thursday 17th September: This workshop is held in a different venue today from 2pm to 4pm. No class at 8am.

What do other disciplines have to offer in providing services to people with a disability? How can social workers collaborate with allied health professionals? In this workshop we will discuss this and more with professionals from other disciplines.

This is a compulsory face to face workshop related to the first assessment. You will be working with Speech Pathology students on a case study.

There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the practice response assessment.

Learning outcomes: L04

No class at 8am. What do other disciplines have to offer in providing services to people with a disability? How can social workers collaborate with allied health professionals? In this workshop we will discuss this and more with professionals from other disciplines. This is a compulsory face to face workshop related to the first assessment. You will be working with Speech Pathology students on a case study. There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the practice response assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9: Disability in the Global South

Thursday, 24th September: This workshop will examine different cultural understandings and experiences of disability. We will look at the connection between the cycle of poverty and disability and understand the problem of applying a purely Eurocentric view to disability studies.

We will conclude the session with your fifth of seven (5/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade). It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content. Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

We will conclude the session with your fifth of seven (5/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade).  It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content.  Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 10: Accessiblity and co-production

Thursday 8th October: This workshop will be co-produced with people with intellectual disabilities. We will consider ways in which services and businesses can become more welcoming to people with disabilities. We will also be developing our skills in implementing Easy English. This workshop will prepare students for the final assessment.

There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the Easy English assessment.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04

This workshop will prepare students for the final assessment. There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the Easy English assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11: Disabilty Advocacy 1

Thursday 15th October: Today's workshop will explore individual models of disability advocacy and introduce students to Wolfensberger's well-known definition of social advocacy.

We will conclude the session with your sixth of seven (6/7) in-class quizzes (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade). It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content. Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content.  Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12: Disability Advocacy 2

Thursday 22nd October: Today we will continue exploring disability advocacy, in particular systemic advocacy. We will explore different tools and strategies that can influence policy and practice for groups of people with disabilities.

We will conclude the session with your final (7/7) in-class quiz (15 mins; 4-5 questions; 5% of total grade). It will assess your understanding of material from the required readings and workshop for this week's content. Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

Remember - only the best 6 scores out of the 7 quizzes will be counted towards your total mark for this assessment item.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 13: Disability informed social work

Thursday 29th October: What does it take to achieve social inclusion for people with a disability? How can social workers help people with a disability to become active and welcome citizens in their local communities? Some time also spent on reviewing the semester and assistance with final assessment.

There will be no quiz today - we'll be focusing on what you need to do for the Easy English assessment.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
